expectations.text: AGENTS.md is created at the monorepo root
expectations.evidence: outputs/AGENTS.md exists and contains a well-structured root-level AGENTS.md with a Packages table, Cross-Package Knowledge section, Hard Constraints, and Task Routing.

expectations.text: Root CLAUDE.md is a symlink to AGENTS.md

expectations.evidence: outputs/packages/api/AGENTS.md exists and contains @platform/api documentation with Knowledge Base, Hard Constraints, and Task Routing sections.

expectations.text: packages/api/CLAUDE.md is a symlink to packages/api/AGENTS.md
Root AGENTS.md references or links to per-package AGENTS.md files
expectations.evidence: Root AGENTS.md Packages table has '[→](packages/api/AGENTS.md)' for packages/api, and Task Routing includes 'Working inside packages/api? Start at [packages/api/AGENTS.md](packages/api/AGENTS.md)'.

expectations.text: packages/api/knowledge/ is created with at least one doc
expectations.evidence: outputs/packages/api/knowledge/ contains 3 documents: auth-token-verification.md, graphql-schema-design.md, and rate-limiting.md.

expectations.text: Root AGENTS.md covers cross-package concerns; api AGENTS.md focuses on api-specific content
expectations.evidence: Root AGENTS.md covers shared type contracts, auth boundaries, coordinated change protocol, and cross-package routing. packages/api/AGENTS.md focuses exclusively on JWT verification, GraphQL schema design, rate limiting, and resolver constraints, while correctly linking back to root for cross-package concerns (e.g., '../../knowledge/shared-contracts.md').

claims.claim: Root AGENTS.md references two cross-package knowledge docs: shared-contracts.md and auth-boundaries.md
claims.evidence: Root AGENTS.md Cross-Package Knowledge table lists 'knowledge/shared-contracts.md' and 'knowledge/auth-boundaries.md'. The outputs/knowledge/ directory exists confirming these are real output files.

claims.claim: packages/api/AGENTS.md correctly cross-references root-level knowledge docs using relative paths
claims.evidence: packages/api/AGENTS.md uses '../../knowledge/shared-contracts.md' and '../../knowledge/auth-boundaries.md', demonstrating accurate relative navigation to root-level cross-package docs.

claims.claim: Hard constraints in both AGENTS.md files are meaningful and specific
claims.evidence: Root: 'Never change a type in packages/shared without updating both packages/api and packages/web in the same release cycle.' api: 'All resolvers must read identity (userId, role) from request context — never directly from headers.' Both are concrete and enforceable.

suggestions.assertion: packages/api/knowledge/ is created with at least one doc
suggestions.reason: Passes trivially with 1 empty file. The assertion does not check whether the knowledge docs have meaningful content. Consider requiring at least N lines of substantive content or verifying coverage of expected topics (e.g., auth, schema, rate limiting).

No assertion checks the root-level knowledge/ directory. Root AGENTS.md references knowledge/shared-contracts.md and knowledge/auth-boundaries.md — if those files were absent the links would be broken and the output would be misleading. An assertion like 'root knowledge/ contains at least one doc' would catch this.

No assertion verifies that packages/api/AGENTS.md back-links to the root AGENTS.md for cross-package navigation. This bidirectional linking is present in the output and is an important quality signal, but is not checked.

eval_feedback.overall: Core structure checks are solid. Main gaps: no assertion covers the root knowledge/ directory (referenced but unchecked), and file-existence checks don't verify doc content quality.
